smart_contracts: Update decimals to 18 for ERC-20 standard compliance
Replace single bankOperator with Multi-Sig/Governance control
backend_infrastructure: Replace in-memory storage with PostgreSQL persistence
Implement ACID transactions for fiat ledger
Implement row-level locking to prevent race conditions/double-spending
Introduce a Nonce Manager for blockchain transactions
Remove hardcoded client mappings in BesuClient
orchestration: Transition from simple try-except Saga to persistent state machine (e.g., Temporal or Celery)
Implement a persistent message broker for guaranteed compensation
testing: Add integration tests using a live Besu node
Implement adversarial testing (network partitions, node failures, gas exhaustion)

details: Defined relational schema for Accounts, Transactions, and AuditLogs
Added PostgreSQL service to docker-compose.yml with healthchecks
Implemented database seeding for initial customer accounts (Alice, Bob)
Implemented SELECT FOR UPDATE row-level locking in FiatLedger to prevent race conditions during balance updates
Refactored FastAPI endpoints to use scoped database sessions via Dependencies
Updated lifecycle management to initialize and seed DB on startup

smart_contracts.reason: Focus was on backend persistence first; contract changes require redeployment and state migration.

nonce_management.reason: Not yet implemented; currently relies on sequential nonces which will fail under high concurrency.

distributed_saga.reason: Requires introduction of additional infrastructure (Redis/RabbitMQ/Temporal) which exceeds the scope of the persistence update.

integration_testing.reason: Testing suite currently relies on mocks; requires updated test environment to include the new DB service.

client_mapping.reason: Fiat accounts are now in DB, but on-chain address mappings in BesuClient remain hardcoded.
